title: Debug: Part 1
summary: Classify syntax, runtime, logic, and timeout symptoms.
refresher: A syntax error blocks parsing, a runtime error interrupts execution, and a logic error finishes with the wrong result. Reproduce the failure and change one cause at a time.

blocks.body: A syntax error blocks parsing, a runtime error interrupts execution, and a logic error finishes with the wrong result. Reproduce the failure and change one cause at a time. Classify syntax, runtime, logic, and timeout symptoms.

Debugging starts with classification. A syntax problem prevents parsing, a runtime problem interrupts execution, a timeout suggests repetition that never ends, and a wrong answer means the program completed with incorrect state. Choosing the family narrows the next question you should ask.

blocks.caption: Subtracting one converts the item count into the final valid index.
Use the collection count to compute its final valid index.

blocks.body: For two rooms, length is two but the valid indexes are zero and one. The wrong line reads index two and receives undefined. A useful debugging log would show both `rooms.length` and the index immediately before access.

blocks.title: Trace this carefully
blocks.body: A stack trace can include library frames; start with the first frame naming your source file and inspect that line.

blocks.prompt: What family is a missing parenthesis?
blocks.why: The parser cannot complete the call, so execution never begins.

choices: index two is past the end
blocks.why: A two-item array has indexes zero and one, making two out of bounds.
